Added direct coverage for invalid input with known effective/baseline values and inherited effective/baseline detail rendering. Reused resolve_row as the sole index-to-row owner for stable private row identity and matched sparse override intent once. Rejected removing ConfigUiRow or flattening structured-detail helpers because those changes would weaken custom-renderer safety or special-TOML completeness for little reduction. Full default/no-default/all-feature tests, strict Clippy lanes, rustdoc, formatting, and diff checks pass. A second independent pass traced remaining state/default terminology to the inferred-TOML table and then confirmed ratconfig-h7d.6 explicitly owns that metadata rename together with baseline_toml and evidence-only snapshot semantics. The partial label-only edit was reverted to avoid splitting that contract or creating an intermediate API; no additional h7d.5 defect remained. Rejected rewriting the historical 5.0 release note, broadening search, removing ConfigUiRow, or flattening structured-detail handling because each would either alter unrelated behavior or weaken an existing boundary without a current defect. The restored h7d.5 tree passes formatting, all 116 default tests, and diff checks; its previously recorded no-default/all-feature, strict Clippy, and rustdoc lanes remain green.
close_reason: Implemented truthful sparse presentation: list values prioritize invalid input, effective resolution, explicit intent, then unresolved; details keep intent, override/input, effective, baseline, origins, and external management distinct while deduplicating equal structured payloads by reference. Reset UI uses the shared authorization query, describes removing the override, and previews known baseline or unknown inheritance. Blocking diagnostics retain the real snapshot intent, every exact-field diagnostic is appended, selected-row headers remain source/action-derived, and custom detail callbacks have a total ConfigUiRow resolver. Removed the obsolete baseline-presence helper. Formatting, 116 default tests, 80 no-default tests, 121 all-feature tests, both strict Clippy lanes, rustdoc with warnings denied, and diff checks pass.

title: Make inferred TOML rows evidence-only
description:
## Outcome

Generic TOML inspection exposes only facts observed in the supplied documents. Parsing a current or baseline document must not imply a complete schema, semantic constraints, public status, apply timing, or safe writability.

## Scope

- Preserve observed paths, deterministic order/grouping, literal values, and syntactic shape from the document where each observation came from, including entries seen only in the baseline document.
- Rename the helper input and list metadata from default to baseline. Supplying `baseline_toml` is the host’s assertion that the document represents resolution after removing this source override; Ratconfig does not discover or universalize that baseline.
- Project snapshots conservatively: a current-document entry is Explicit intent but does not by itself populate effective resolution; a baseline-only entry is Absent with identical effective/baseline values under the host’s baseline assertion; an entry present in both remains Explicit even when equal, carries the observed baseline, and leaves effective unknown. Make every inferred row read-only unless the host explicitly enriches it with an approved editor capability.
- Return no invented fields for an invalid current document. Replace the opaque string error with a typed error that identifies whether the current or baseline document failed and preserves the parser message; the host maps it to an appropriate diagnostic and retains its independently declared exact file action.
- Inferred read-only fields do not guess a file action from source counts. The host may attach an exact capability file_action_id when it owns that mapping.
- Avoid fabricating field recovery when the whole document cannot be parsed safely.
- Update ConfigUiTomlDocumentSpec (`default_toml` to `baseline_toml`), build_toml_document_fields, list-table metadata/column labels, docs, and focused tests to the new ownership contract.

## Verification

Tests prove that observed scalar syntax alone does not authorize editing, current-only and current-plus-baseline rows never claim effective resolution from literal presence, explicit-equal-baseline remains explicit, baseline-only observations obey inherited effective-equals-baseline while retaining their own literal and syntactic shape without claiming inventory completeness, complex values retain faithful previews/file fallback, and invalid current/baseline inputs report the correct typed side, with an invalid current document producing no fields and remaining mappable by the host to a source diagnostic with its existing action. Full Ratconfig quality gates pass.

## Stop conditions

Stop if Ratconfig begins mirroring a product schema, guessing enums/ranges/units/apply behavior, or displacing the native file as canonical storage.
notes: Implemented evidence-only inferred TOML rows in model.rs with one host-neutral owner. ConfigUiTomlDocumentSpec accepts host-asserted baseline_toml with no compatibility alias. Current entries are Explicit with unknown effective resolution; current-plus-baseline entries remain Explicit even when equal and carry the observed baseline; baseline-only entries are Absent with identical effective and baseline resolution. The table is intent/value/baseline with blank missing-baseline cells. Every inferred row is ReadOnly, has no guessed file_action_id, cannot unset, and preserves host validation text exactly. ConfigUiTomlDocumentError distinguishes Current and Baseline failures while preserving parser messages. Simplification passes shared override_intent_label; removed an infallible Result layer, a single-use parse wrapper, duplicate error formatting, repeated assertions, fixture duplication, and redundant type annotation; borrowed parsed observations instead of deep-cloning nested values; matched current presence once for intent/effective; directly mapped successful parses; and reduced patchability to the same short-circuit predicate. Tests distinguish validation from edit authority and syntactic classification from schema inference, and directly assert source-file fallback for quoted and inline-table paths. README distinguishes schema-authorized mutation from exact file routing on a row that remains read-only. Rejected tolerant recovery, inferred schemas/constraints/effective values, automatic action lookup, product provenance, default_toml compatibility, deleting patchability guidance or structured-TOML fallback, collapsing typed errors, and per-channel rendering metadata because those would fabricate evidence, weaken contracts, or add a behavior-changing model surface. Final verification passes 118 default tests, 82 no-default tests, 123 all-feature tests, both strict Clippy lanes, rustdoc with warnings denied, formatting, and diff checks.
close_reason: Implemented evidence-only inferred TOML rows, host-asserted baseline input, typed side-aware parse errors, truthful intent/value/baseline metadata, and read-only authority; the full Ratconfig quality matrix passes.

title: Publish sparse-intent Ratconfig and migrate Yazelix
description:
## Outcome

Ship the parent architecture as one intentional breaking Ratconfig release and migrate the only current consumer without compatibility shims.

## Scope

- Confirm every child contract is complete, no intermediate child API was published or consumed, and no replaced config-UI Core/current/default/kind-authorization surface, BeginEdit intent, row-index intent or editor payload, ConfigUiPathOwner, the hard-coded `advanced` branch or schema-tab injection, public mutable app internals, or index-based completion helper remains. Do not delete unrelated migration/default or contract/schema metadata.
- Publish the already-reserved 6.0.0 major: update Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock, README examples, rewrite the existing 6.0.0 unreleased notes to the final contract, and tag v6.0.0; include already-unreleased cursor editing changes in the same release when appropriate.
- Coordinate with the existing downstream bead `yazelix-5vl-core-all-config-discovery-lsw.13`; do not create a duplicate. Once its worktree is free, reconcile that bead before implementation: change wait-for-publication ordering to this local-release-candidate preflight transaction; include field-diagnosed rows in Overview; adopt the host-selected operational tab, validated/private app boundary, stable intents, `baseline_toml`, and evidence-only effective-value rules; and preserve its existing Yazelix ownership constraints. Before tagging, prove its adapter migration against the exact committed local Ratconfig release candidate in a clean/disposable Yazelix checkout or after the current worktree owner finishes; do not mutate an actively owned dirty checkout. Only after that focused consumer preflight passes, publish/tag Ratconfig, replace the temporary local dependency with the exact pushed git revision and Nix hash, and rerun Yazelix without overrides.
- Migrate the Yazelix adapter to validated sparse snapshots, recommendations including every field-diagnosed row, capabilities, origins, generic owner labels, reset/unset, diagnostics, a host-selected operational tab, evidence-only native rows with host-asserted baseline inputs and no inferred effective resolution for explicit document entries, and one real ConfigUiSource per field source including Yazi config and theme sources. Update Yazelix current-state documentation, CHANGELOG, and README LOC scorecard for the user-visible/runtime and production-LOC changes. Field-invalid parsers must supply Invalid input rather than relying on diagnostics to synthesize intent.
- Use ConfigUiApp::try_new, read-only app accessors, and fallible model replacement; replace selected_config_source tab lookup and public raw-model row queries with validated app behavior. After a successful host write and reload, use the atomic validated replacement/completion path keyed by ConfigUiFieldId so a committed edit is cleared without a false cancellation even if the new model removes the field or changes its authority/capability; route file actions by unique `(source_id, action_id)` while retaining the validated emitted path/create payload; preserve selected field/action identity across reload reordering; and never use a stale row index or emitted value as committed state, including for theme resolution. Preserve and correctly rebind the staged edit on host failure or model reordering.
- Run Ratconfig checks plus Yazelix focused/Nix integration checks and manually dogfood Overview/All, editing, reset, invalid input, field diagnostics without a forced operational tab, the populated operational tab, external editor, and native-file fallback.
- Close the parent only after the published pin is consumed and the runtime path is verified.

## Verification

Ratconfig format/test/all-feature/no-default-feature/Clippy/rustdoc/diff gates pass; a focused Yazelix adapter preflight passes against the exact local release candidate before publication, including successful-write reload with a changed capability and failed-write edit preservation; the tag resolves to that pushed release commit; Yazelix then builds without local overrides, its docs/CHANGELOG/LOC scorecard agree, and its installed or temporary-profile runtime exercises the new UI contract.

## Stop conditions

Stop before tagging or cross-repo edits if another agent owns the consumer worktree, publication approval is missing, or the consumer cannot represent explicit-same-as-baseline and capability authorization truthfully.
notes: Integration ordering invariant: after a failed set/unset, install the host failure notice before any ordinary replacement of a reloaded model so a valid replacement can compose an edit-cancellation notice instead of erasing either condition; when the edit remains compatible, preserve it for retry. After a successful write, use the atomic validated replacement/completion operation keyed by field identity. It must ingest committed model truth and clear the matching edit as committed without routing that same edit through ordinary incompatibility cancellation.

title: Make u a truthful reset-to-inherited keybinding
description:
## Why

Ratconfig already maps normal-mode `u` to an unset intent, but the current implementation exposes it only when a baseline value is known. That confuses preview data with write authorization: an explicit or invalid override can be safely removed even when the host cannot predict the inherited result.

## Evidence

- `src/editor.rs:657` binds normal-mode `u` to the reset path.
- `src/editor.rs:955-974` currently gates `UnsetField` on `has_baseline_value()` and reports no default when the baseline is unknown.
- `src/render.rs:1441-1446` exposes the key hint only when a baseline exists.
- The locked `ratconfig-h7d.1` contract defines `ConfigUiField::can_unset` and makes unset available exactly for Explicit or Invalid intent, `can_unset == true`, and a writable source; baseline knowledge is preview-only.
- `ratconfig-h7d.3` introduces capability-driven editing while deliberately keeping unset authorization separate.

## Scope

- Keep `u` as the normal-mode reset key and route it through one validated reset-availability query.
- Make reset available exactly when the selected field has Explicit or Invalid intent, declares `can_unset`, and belongs to a writable source.
- Keep reset independent of editor capability: a read-only editor capability may still allow unset, while an editable field may forbid it.
- Emit `ConfigUiIntent::UnsetField` with stable `ConfigUiFieldId`; remove remaining index-based reset routing.
- Allow unset with an unknown baseline. Never emit `SetField` with a baseline copy and never fabricate the inherited result.
- Do nothing for non-field rows, Absent intent, unauthorized unset, or read-only source authority; surface a concise notice where existing interaction conventions require one.
- Keep `u` inert in search and staged-edit modes so typed input and Ctrl+u editing behavior remain unchanged.
- Leave inherited-value prose, preview rendering, and final control-line wording to `ratconfig-h7d.5`.

## Implementation Notes

- Reuse the public `can_unset` field and source-authority validation introduced by `ratconfig-h7d.3`; do not add reset to `ConfigUiCapability`.
- Centralize availability so the reducer and renderer cannot disagree.
- After host persistence, use the existing atomic validated replacement/completion path keyed by field identity; committed truth comes only from the reloaded model.
- Add no host callbacks, default-resolution logic, or product-specific policy.

## Verification

- `u` on an Explicit field with `can_unset` and a writable source emits stable-identity `UnsetField`.
- The same works for Invalid intent, a ReadOnly capability, and an unknown baseline.
- Absent intent, `can_unset == false`, read-only source, non-field rows, search mode, and staged editing emit no unset; construction and replacement continue rejecting a field whose source is missing.
- A known baseline is never copied into a `SetField` intent.
- Host rejection can preserve state for retry, while successful atomic reload shows the host-supplied inherited state without false edit cancellation.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`, `cargo test`, and `git diff --check` pass.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if reset requires Ratconfig to compute defaults or resolution policy.
- Stop if the implementation couples unset authorization to editor capability, type metadata, baseline presence, or rendered syntax.
- Stop if removing an override cannot be represented as a stable-identity `UnsetField` intent.
- Stop before adding a second reset key or a compatibility path; `u` is the approved normal-mode binding.

## Maintenance Notes

This bead owns reset authorization and key dispatch. `ratconfig-h7d.5` owns the truthful sparse-state presentation and inherited-value preview; Yazelix owns persistence, reload, and apply behavior.
close_reason: Implemented truthful reset-to-inherited authorization: normal-mode u and its footer hint share one stable-identity query based on override intent, can_unset, and writable source; unknown baselines and read-only edit capabilities remain independently supported, with regression coverage across modes and host reload/retry behavior.

title: Collapse negligible Overview distinctions
description:
## Why

A dogfood report showed a settings tab labeled `Core 8/9`: the reduced view hid only one field while most of the list pane remained empty. In Ratconfig 6 terminology, any `overview < total` count currently creates an Overview/All distinction, even when the reduction is too small to justify another mode and shortcut.

The problem is not insufficient viewport filling. Host recommendations are semantic and must remain stable across terminal sizes. The generic UI needs a minimum-benefit policy so negligible distinctions collapse without turning Overview into an arbitrary first-N list.

## Evidence

- `src/model.rs:136-185`: Overview visibility and counts include host recommendations plus explicit, invalid, externally managed, and exactly diagnosed fields.
- `src/editor.rs:190-201,414-430,991-996`: any tab with one non-Overview field can select Overview and advertise/toggle All.
- `src/render.rs:480-516`: the title renders the Overview/total distinction whenever the counts differ.
- `../yazelix/crates/yzx-config/src/catalog.rs:49-62` and `../yazelix/crates/yzx-config/src/model.rs:113-122`: the current consumer supplies a semantic allowlist; it does not and should not know Ratconfig viewport height.
- Dogfood reproduction: a nine-field tab with eight Overview-visible rows displays `Core 8/9` and hides one row despite abundant vertical space.

## Decision

Ratconfig owns whether an Overview/All distinction is worthwhile after applying host recommendations and automatic attention visibility. Hosts continue to own which identities they recommend.

A tab has a meaningful distinction only when Overview hides both:

- at least three field rows; and
- at least 25% of that tab's field inventory.

When either condition is false, that tab behaves as All: every field is visible, the title is plain `settings`, and `a` is not advertised or active. The rule is based on validated model counts, never terminal dimensions, field order, or arbitrary backfill. File actions and operational rows do not affect the threshold. Search continues to span All.

Examples:

- 8/9 collapses to all nine fields.
- 9/12 remains Overview because three fields and 25% are hidden.
- 10/13 collapses because three hidden fields are less than 25%.
- A model starts in Overview only if at least one tab has a meaningful distinction.

## User-visible Contract

Small or nearly complete tabs do not expose a second mode that saves only one or two rows. Meaningfully reduced tabs retain stable Overview/All behavior. Switching tabs, resizing, searching, model replacement, diagnostics, and sparse intent changes must never invent recommendation order or lose selection safety.

## Scope

In scope:
- `src/model.rs`: one private meaningful-distinction predicate over field counts.
- `src/editor.rs`: initial view, effective per-tab visibility, toggle eligibility, and replacement reconciliation.
- `src/render.rs`: titles, empty-state wording, and controls derived from the same predicate.
- `README.md`: document the minimum-benefit Overview policy and include it in the unreleased 6.0 contract.
- A few strong model/editor/renderer tests covering threshold boundaries and attention-expanded Overview rows.

Out of scope:
- Changing host recommendation identities or child-tool semantics.
- Reading terminal height to classify fields.
- Backfilling from catalog order, alphabetical order, or first-N selection.
- A cross-tab dashboard, new tabs, configurable thresholds, or public policy objects.
- Yazelix adapter implementation or compatibility with the removed Core API.

## Implementation Notes

1. Give the threshold one private owner and reuse it for model-wide initial view, selected-tab visibility, titles, empty states, and footer controls.
2. Preserve the saved Overview preference across tabs: a negligible tab projects All without forcing a meaningful tab to forget Overview.
3. Compute the rule from the actual Overview field count, including explicit, invalid, externally managed, and exactly diagnosed fields.
4. Keep search behavior unchanged and count fields only.
5. Prefer strengthening existing Overview contracts over adding mirror tests for threshold literals.

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` exits 0.
- `cargo test` exits 0.
- `cargo test --no-default-features` exits 0.
- `cargo test --features crossterm-runner` exits 0.
- Ratatui/model contracts prove 8/9 collapses, 9/12 remains distinct, 10/13 collapses, active search spans All, and tab changes preserve the saved Overview preference.
- A model-replacement contract proves newly explicit or diagnosed rows can collapse a now-negligible distinction without invalid selection or edit state.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the change requires Ratconfig to rank host fields or understand Yazelix/product semantics.
- Stop if correct behavior requires viewport-dependent model state or a public configurable policy API.
- Stop if the implementation mutates `recommended_fields` or creates a second source of Overview membership.
- Preserve any unrelated uncommitted choice-viewport or shortcut-help work in overlapping files.

## Maintenance Notes

Effort: S-M. Risk: MED. Confidence: HIGH.

Rejected alternatives: viewport-driven filling, fixed first-N backfill, always showing All, and a new cross-tab Overview dashboard. The threshold is a generic presentation policy; semantic recommendations remain host-owned.

title: Decide runner helper public API before release
description:
## Why

The optional crossterm runner recently added several public helper functions around key/event conversion and reducer dispatch. They are useful for tests and embedding, but because `src/lib.rs` re-exports the entire runner module when the feature is enabled, each helper becomes public API. If those helpers are not intended as a long-term API surface, trimming them before publication would reduce semver burden and code surface.

## Evidence

- `src/crossterm_runner.rs:45-76`: four public helpers expose key conversion and dispatch: `crossterm_key_to_config_ui_key`, `crossterm_event_to_config_ui_key`, `handle_crossterm_key`, and `handle_crossterm_event`
- `src/crossterm_runner.rs:79-106`: the actual host-facing runner entrypoints are `run_config_ui` and `run_config_ui_with_details`
- `src/lib.rs:20-21`: `pub use crossterm_runner::*` re-exports all public runner symbols when `crossterm-runner` is enabled
- `README.md:98-119`: the documented runner example imports and uses only `run_config_ui`, not the lower-level conversion or dispatch helpers

## Scope

In scope:
- Decide whether the lower-level runner helpers are intentionally public, `pub(crate)`, private, or should be exposed under a narrower module path
- If trimming is accepted, update tests to call private helpers from the module test scope or assert behavior through the remaining public entrypoints where practical
- Keep the documented `run_config_ui` and `run_config_ui_with_details` flow stable unless the maintainer explicitly chooses a breaking API cleanup
- Update README only if the public runner surface changes

Out of scope:
- Do not change editor reducer key semantics
- Do not change terminal initialization/restoration behavior
- Do not add a compatibility shim for a surface that has not been published
- Do not move host validation, file writes, or apply policy into Ratconfig

## Implementation Notes

1. First determine whether this crate version has been consumed externally in a way that makes the helper API effectively committed
2. If not committed, prefer the smallest public API that supports the README example and expected host embedding use
3. Keep `crossterm_key_to_config_ui_key` public only if direct key conversion is a deliberate integration contract; otherwise make conversion/dispatch helpers private or `pub(crate)`
4. Preserve tests for key conversion and ignored events even if the helpers become private
5. If the API is intentionally kept, add a note or docs sentence explaining the intended low-level embedding use so future agents do not rediscover this as accidental slop

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` should exit 0
- `cargo test --features crossterm-runner` should exit 0 and include runner tests
- `cargo clippy --features crossterm-runner -- -D warnings` should exit 0
- `cargo test` should exit 0 for the default feature set
- `git diff --check` should exit 0

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the helper API has already been published or consumed and no explicit breaking-change approval is available
- Stop if deleting helpers requires weakening key conversion tests
- Stop if the cleanup changes reducer behavior, terminal lifecycle behavior, or host callback error propagation
- Stop if the only possible implementation adds more wrapper code than it removes

## Maintenance Notes

This is not automatically isomorphic: reducing public API can be behavior-changing for downstream callers. Dependency decision before implementation: add no new crates. Build in-house by changing visibility/export boundaries only. Packaging impact: no dependency or runtime closure growth; potential semver/API impact must be called out explicitly before landing.
notes: Implementation decision: no new crates. Treat lower-level crossterm conversion/dispatch helpers as accidental public API because README documents only run_config_ui and run_config_ui_with_details, and the runner commits are still local/ahead of origin in this branch. Keep the test coverage by using private helpers from the module test scope. Preserve terminal lifecycle, reducer semantics, and host callback error propagation.
close_reason: Completed: trimmed accidental lower-level crossterm runner helper API before publication by making key/event conversion and event dispatch helpers private, deleting the now-unused handle_crossterm_key wrapper, and keeping the documented run_config_ui/run_config_ui_with_details entrypoints stable. Runner tests still cover key conversion, ignored release/non-key events, and dispatch through the private event helper. Verified with cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo test --features crossterm-runner, cargo clippy --features crossterm-runner -- -D warnings, cargo test, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo test --no-default-features, cargo clippy --no-default-features -- -D warnings, and git diff --check.

title: Use Space to stage and Enter to save booleans
description: Refine the staged boolean interaction chosen in ratconfig-gpj: normal-mode Space stages the opposite boolean value, Enter while editing emits SetField, and Esc cancels. Normal-mode Enter on a boolean must not toggle or open a no-op edit. Other fields, file actions, and host-owned persistence remain unchanged. This follows the maintainer's explicit UX decision that Space then Enter is clearer than double Enter.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Space on a normal-mode boolean stages the opposite value and emits no persistence intent.
- Enter on a normal-mode boolean leaves the value/edit state unchanged and gives concise control guidance.
- Enter while the boolean edit is staged emits the existing SetField intent; Esc discards it.
- Non-boolean fields and file-action activation retain their current Enter/Space behavior.
- Normal reducer, rendering/control text, and optional crossterm-runner tests defend the interaction.
- Ratconfig format, default/all-feature/no-default-feature tests, and clippy gates pass before publication.
- Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock and README dependency example declare 2.0.0, and the published release is tagged v2.0.0 before downstream consumers update.
notes:
Dependency decision before implementation:
- Production crates: unchanged.
- Dev-only crates: unchanged.
- In-house logic: one reducer guard and control wording using existing ConfigUiEditState/ConfigUiIntent; no new public type, state machine, or host API.
- Rejected alternatives: double-Enter staging, immediate Space persistence, a global save queue, or a compatibility option supporting both interactions.
- Packaging impact: the documented stable contract treats observable reducer changes as major, so release Ratconfig 2.0.0/v2.0.0. Then publish aligned cursor and Next revisions before updating main Yazelix's Cargo pin/Nix hash and rerunning the no-override package gate.

SemVer classification before implementation:
- This changes documented normal-mode reducer semantics from Enter-or-Space staging to Space-only staging.
- Ratconfig's version policy explicitly names observable reducer-semantic changes as major. Version 2.0.0 is therefore required even though the source diff is small.
close_reason: Completed and released: normal-mode Space stages the opposite boolean without persistence, normal-mode Enter leaves the value/edit state unchanged with guidance, staged Enter emits SetField, and Esc cancels; non-boolean and file-action behavior is preserved. Ratconfig metadata, lockfile, README dependency example, and release notes declare 2.0.0; commit 110e749 is pushed and tagged v2.0.0. Verified cargo fmt, default/all-feature/no-default-feature tests, all-target clippy in all three feature modes with -D warnings, and git diff --check.

title: Make Ratconfig's host model source-first
description:
## Why

Ratconfig added generic per-source metadata for multi-file hosts, but ConfigUiModel still requires legacy single-config and cursor-specific paths. This leaves two ownership models in the public API, makes every host populate stale fields, and causes unmapped tabs to display the legacy active-config header.

## Evidence

- `src/model.rs:13-20`: ConfigUiModel carries `active_config_path`, two cursor-specific paths, existence/owner/write flags, and the newer generic `sources` collection together.
- `src/render.rs:214-229`: selected-source metadata is preferred, but any unmapped tab falls back to the legacy active-config fields.
- `README.md:51-79`: the minimal generic host example must populate empty cursor paths and single-config metadata even though sources are the reusable abstraction.
- `../yazelix-next/crates/yzn-config/src/model.rs:103-160`: Nova supplies empty legacy cursor paths while separately describing every real document through ConfigUiSource.
- `../yazelix/rust_core/yazelix_core/src/config_ui/model_builder.rs:257-280`: Classic also supplies both legacy model metadata and generic sources.

## Architecture Outcome

ConfigUiModel is source-first: generic ConfigUiSource metadata is the sole owner of config-document identity, path, existence, ownership, and write mode. Tabs without a source use an explicit neutral header state rather than implicitly borrowing an unrelated root config. Cursor-specific paths disappear from Ratconfig.

## Scope

In scope:
- Simplify ConfigUiModel, selected_config_source, and header rendering around generic sources.
- Remove the legacy active-config and cursor-specific model fields in an intentional major Ratconfig release.
- Update Ratconfig examples and focused model/render tests.
- Migrate both current consumers, Classic Yazelix and Nova, in coordinated pinned-revision transactions.
- Measure Ratconfig plus consumer production LOC before and after; the new shape must reduce combined ownership.

Out of scope:
- No schema loading, validation, config discovery, file IO, atomic writes, migrations, editor launching, or apply policy in Ratconfig.
- No Yazelix, Nova, Mars, Zellij, Yazi, cursor, Home Manager, or command-name policy in Ratconfig types.
- No compatibility shim that keeps both source metadata and the legacy model fields indefinitely.
- No redesign of tabs, diagnostics, file actions, or the config editor.

## Implementation Notes

1. Inventory all ConfigUiModel construction sites and any external published consumers before changing the public contract.
2. Define explicit rendering behavior for source-backed tabs and source-less operational tabs.
3. Delete the legacy fields and fallback branch rather than adding a third document abstraction.
4. Update Ratconfig tests and README to demonstrate a neutral non-Yazelix multi-source host.
5. Publish the Ratconfig major revision, then update Classic and Nova pins and construction sites.
6. Record the before/after production LOC across Ratconfig and both hosts.

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` should exit 0.
- `cargo test` should exit 0 with focused source-backed and source-less header tests.
- `cargo test --all-features` should exit 0.
- `cargo test --no-default-features` should exit 0.
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` should exit 0.
- `rg -n "active_config_path|cursor_config_path|default_cursor_config_path|active_config_exists|config_owner|config_read_only" src README.md` should find no retained legacy public model ownership.
- Classic and Nova focused config-UI tests should pass against pushed Ratconfig revisions without local dependency overrides.
- The recorded combined production LOC should decrease.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the change starts moving host persistence, schema, or runtime policy into Ratconfig.
- Stop if source-less tabs cannot be represented neutrally without a broader UI redesign.
- Stop if a compatibility layer would leave both old and new ownership models live.
- Stop if current external consumers make a major release unacceptable until the maintainer chooses a migration policy.
- Stop if combined production LOC does not decrease.

## Maintenance Notes

- Priority P3: this is post-convergence API cleanup, not a cursor-work dependency or Nova cutover gate.
- Effort M; implementation risk MED; confidence HIGH.
- Dependency decision: add no production or dev crates; use existing model and renderer primitives.
- SemVer: removing public ConfigUiModel fields is a major change. Batch it with other accepted host-API cleanup instead of creating repeated major releases.
- Rejected alternative: retain the legacy fields as permanent fallback metadata. That preserves the duplicate ownership this bead exists to remove.
notes: Implementation checkpoint 2026-07-12: Ratconfig 3.0.0 removes the six legacy ConfigUiModel document fields, ConfigUiSource is the only model owner, and source-less tabs render neutral not-file-backed metadata. Classic routes edit paths through ConfigUiSource and resolves packaged cursor defaults/apply inputs from its host request. Nova drops the six stale constructor fields. Scoped production LOC across Ratconfig model/render, Classic model-builder/app/apply adapter, and Nova model fell from 6124 to 6103 (-21). Verified Ratconfig fmt, default/all-features/no-default-features tests, clippy -D warnings, zero legacy refs in Ratconfig src/README, Nova yzn-config 38 tests, Classic 35 config_ui tests plus the Home Manager integration test through a temporary local Ratconfig override. Remaining release transaction: manual approval, publish Ratconfig, update Classic's pinned git revision, rerun Classic without overrides, and commit consumer integrations. Concurrent Nova work owns unrelated yzn-config/Beads changes; preserve it.
close_reason: Completed in 901f6e1 and released in Ratconfig v3.0.0 (80c9329). ConfigUiSource is the sole document-metadata owner, source-less tabs render neutral metadata, legacy model fields are absent, and the recorded Ratconfig, Classic, and Nova checks and combined LOC reduction passed. Main Yazelix integrated the published Ratconfig 3 API and closed its consumer bead in 5f6a6259; it now pins current Ratconfig 36375ca.

title: Remove Yazelix schema extension hard-code from ratconfig
description:
## Why

Ratconfig is now a project-agnostic child crate, but `src/model.rs` still hard-codes Yazelix's JSON Schema extension key `x-yazelix` when extracting tab order. That keeps a Yazelix-specific schema namespace inside the reusable crate and violates the local boundary that host applications own product-specific schema metadata.

## Evidence

- `AGENTS.md:12`: ratconfig owns project-agnostic Ratatui config editor primitives.
- `AGENTS.md:14`: Yazelix-specific Home Manager, Zellij, Yazi, runtime-refresh, and command-name policy stay out of this crate.
- `README.md:5`: README says Ratconfig is project-agnostic and hosts provide their own config schema and behavior.
- `src/model.rs:317`: `schema_tabs` extracts tabs directly from the schema.
- `src/model.rs:319`: `schema_tabs` hard-codes `.get("x-yazelix")`.
- `src/model.rs:642`: the unit test describes the behavior as reusable, but the fixture uses `"x-yazelix"`.
- Main Yazelix currently calls `schema_tabs(&schema, DEFAULT_TABS)` from `rust_core/yazelix_core/src/config_ui/model_builder.rs:18`, so changing this API requires a main integration update.

## Scope

In scope:
- Remove hard-coded knowledge of `x-yazelix` from ratconfig.
- Either make the schema extension key host-supplied, such as `schema_tabs(schema, extension_key, default_tabs)`, or move Yazelix-specific tab extraction fully into main Yazelix.
- Keep generic fallback behavior and the automatic `advanced` tab policy if it is truly generic; otherwise make that host-supplied too.
- Update ratconfig tests to use a neutral extension key or host-supplied key.
- Coordinate the main Yazelix consumer update in the same release transaction if the public API changes.

Out of scope:
- Do not rename Yazelix's existing `x-yazelix` schema metadata in the main repo unless a main integration bead explicitly decides to.
- Do not move Yazelix config schema, Home Manager ownership, validation, file IO, or apply policy into ratconfig.
- Do not do a broad config UI redesign.

## Implementation Notes

1. Decide whether `advanced` is a generic ratconfig convention or a Yazelix UI convention.
2. If keeping tab extraction in ratconfig, change the API so the host supplies the schema extension key and tests prove a non-Yazelix key works.
3. If moving extraction to main, delete `schema_tabs` from ratconfig and implement the `x-yazelix` read in main Yazelix's config UI adapter.
4. Publish the ratconfig child commit before updating main Yazelix's git dependency.
5. Update main Yazelix to consume the new API or local extraction path and run the coupled release transaction without local overrides.

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` should pass in ratconfig.
- `cargo test` should pass in ratconfig.
- A targeted grep in ratconfig, `rg -n "x-yazelix|Yazelix" src`, should show no source-owned hard-coded Yazelix schema extension references except deliberately generic non-Yazelix test names.
- Main Yazelix focused config UI tests should pass after the dependency update.
- Main Yazelix's Cargo lock should point at the pushed ratconfig commit, not a local override.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the main Yazelix consumer cannot be updated in the same cross-repo transaction after a ratconfig API change.
- Stop if the change starts moving Yazelix-owned schema semantics into ratconfig instead of removing them.

## Maintenance Notes

This is the only concrete extraction leftover found in the ratconfig source audit. Exact old crate/repo names (`yazelix-ratconfig`, `yazelix_ratconfig`, old GitHub URL) were already absent from ratconfig, yazelix-cursors, and main tracked source surfaces.
close_reason: Completed: ratconfig schema_tabs now takes a host-supplied schema extension key instead of hard-coding x-yazelix. Verified with cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo test, rg -n 'x-yazelix|schema_tabs\(' src, and a main Yazelix local override smoke test for cargo test -p yazelix_core config_ui.

title: Make shortcut help contextual and complete
description:
## Why

Ratconfig's footer currently concatenates raw prose such as `q quit Tab tabs j/k move / search`. The lack of semantic separation makes keys and actions read like one sentence, key styling is inconsistent, and the footer can advertise normal-mode commands while search input owns the keyboard. `Ctrl+u` already clears active search and text-edit buffers, but only the text-edit help exposes it. Users need concise contextual guidance in the footer and one complete discoverable shortcut inventory.

## Evidence

- `src/render.rs:1477-1531`: `render_footer` and `footer_control_line` assemble global controls from raw spans and show the same normal controls while search is active.
- `src/render.rs:1620-1682`: normal and edit controls are separate raw strings with inconsistent key/action grouping and styling.
- `src/editor.rs:617-638`: search mode owns character input and supports `Ctrl+u` clear, so normal-mode `q`, `j/k`, Tab, and `/` guidance is misleading while search is active.
- `src/editor.rs:704-722`: text editing also supports `Ctrl+u` clear.
- `src/crossterm_runner.rs:53-79`: crossterm already maps Ctrl-modified characters and ordinary `?` through the existing `ConfigUiKey` vocabulary; no new input dependency or public key variant is required.

## User-visible Contract

The footer renders shortcuts as semantic key/action pairs. Key tokens use the accent color, action labels use normal text, and separators are muted. It shows only commands active in the current context and never presents inactive normal-mode commands during search or text entry.

Representative layouts:

- Normal: `q Quit · Tab Switch · j/k Move · / Search · ? Help`, with view, row action, and inherit hints included only when applicable.
- Search: `search: <query>_` plus `Enter Done · Esc Done · Ctrl+u Clear`.
- Text edit: save, cancel, cursor/edit commands, `Ctrl+u Clear`, and external-editor help.
- Toggle, choice, multichoice, ordered multichoice, file-action, and read-only states show only their usable actions.

Footer fitting is terminal-cell-aware. It keeps hints atomic, preserves the highest-priority safety/action commands, and uses a restrained overflow indication rather than clipping half a key/action pair. When not every binding fits, `? Help` provides the complete inventory.

From normal mode, `?` opens a read-only shortcut help view generated from the same structured hint data. It groups every advertised binding by context, includes conditional actions, uses the same styling, scrolls safely in short terminals, and closes with `Esc` or `?` without changing selection, search, staged edits, notices, or host state. Search and free-text input continue treating `?` as text rather than stealing it for help.

## Scope

In scope:
- Private semantic shortcut descriptors and rendering in `src/render.rs`.
- The smallest private help-view/open/scroll state and reducer routing in `src/editor.rs`.
- Reuse of existing `ConfigUiKey` and crossterm conversion; focused runner coverage only if needed.
- Contexts for normal navigation, Overview/All, applied and active search, file actions, read-only fields, inherit, free text, toggles, choices, multichoices, and ordered multichoices.
- Current-state README documentation for `? Help` and `Ctrl+u Clear`.
- A few strong renderer/reducer contracts covering the context matrix, styling, width fitting, and complete help inventory.

Out of scope:
- User-configurable remapping, a public binding registry, command palette, macros, runtime reflection, or parsing Rust/help strings.
- Host-specific commands or product wording.
- Changing existing key semantics other than adding the private normal/help `?` interaction.
- Capturing `?` during search or free-text entry.
- Mouse interaction or a general overlay framework.
- Duplicating shortcut inventories separately for footer and help.

## Implementation Notes

1. Introduce the smallest private `ControlHint`-style representation containing semantic keys, action text, context, and display priority. Generate dynamic labels such as Overview/All and file-action names from validated app state.
2. Keep reducer behavior authoritative. Footer and help share one presentation inventory derived from the same mode/capability predicates; do not build a generic declarative dispatch framework merely to render help.
3. Separate status/notice text from shortcut hints so an active search visibly owns both footer rows instead of inheriting normal-mode instructions.
4. Render key spans, actions, and separators centrally. Fit complete hints using existing terminal-width helpers; never slice inside a hint.
5. Add one private help view with deterministic section order and minimal scroll state. Reuse the structured hints and keep host callbacks/model state untouched.
6. Strengthen existing narrow-render and reducer tests with a context matrix rather than adding one mirror test per literal key label.

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` exits 0.
- `cargo test` exits 0.
- A Ratatui `TestBackend` contract proves normal, active-search, applied-search, text, toggle, choice, multichoice, and ordered-multichoice footers advertise only active commands; key spans have the accent style; and narrow widths contain only whole prioritized hints.
- The same contract opens `? Help`, verifies every structured binding appears in its correct section, scrolls in a short terminal, and closes without mutating editor state.
- Reducer coverage proves `Ctrl+u` clears active search and text buffers, and `?` is help only outside search/free-text entry.
- `cargo test --no-default-features` exits 0.
- `cargo test --features crossterm-runner` exits 0.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the implementation requires a public keybinding/configuration API or host-specific shortcut metadata.
- Stop and ask before replacing the reducer with a generic command-dispatch framework.
- Do not implement the bead on top of another session's uncommitted choice-viewport work; preserve or wait for that owner.
- If the full help inventory exceeds the viewport, add bounded private scrolling rather than silently clipping entries.

## Maintenance Notes

Effort: M. Risk: MED. Confidence: HIGH.

This should complete before the unfinished 6.0 publication task because it addresses direct release-candidate dogfood feedback and establishes the discoverability contract for the new edit modes.

Rejected alternatives: rendering every binding in the footer, which cannot remain readable at realistic widths; colorizing raw strings, which cannot reliably distinguish keys from prose; duplicating separate footer and help lists; and introducing a public configurable binding framework for a presentation problem.

title: Polish light-mode contrast and pane layout
description:
## Why

Ratconfig's previous pane polish still draws its own left and right rails beside the host Zellij pane border. Runtime dogfooding shows the doubled edge rather than a clean flush surface. Removing the pane rails also exposes a need for deliberate whitespace and a restrained boundary between the tabs and the two content panes.

## Evidence

- User screenshot on 2026-07-15 shows the Ratconfig side rail inset beside Zellij's bright pane border.
- `src/render.rs` gives both settings and details `LEFT | RIGHT` borders and overlaps them to create a shared divider.
- The body begins immediately below the tab row, while list/detail content relies on border-owned inset instead of explicit layout spacing.
- The accepted color and semantic-row fixes remain correct; this follow-up changes composition, not palette ownership.

## User-Visible Contract

Ratconfig draws no vertical rails around either body pane. Tabs are followed by one subtle inset horizontal separator. Settings and details sit below it with aligned headings, a blank row before their content, one-cell horizontal content padding, and a visible center gutter. The resulting surface does not compete with the host pane border, while the two panes and tab/body hierarchy remain immediately legible in light and dark appearance.

## Ratconfig Scope

- Remove left, right, and shared vertical body-pane borders.
- Add explicit one-cell horizontal padding to tabs and each pane's content.
- Add one blank row between each pane heading and its content.
- Add a one-cell center gutter between settings and details.
- Add one subtle horizontal separator below the tabs, inset from both outer edges so it does not form false junctions with host borders.
- Preserve equal pane weighting, settings/details names, field width calculation from actual content width, wrapping, selection, narrow layouts, semantic colors, and dark/light behavior.
- Replace the old rail-geometry contract with focused rendered-buffer coverage for padding, gutter, separator, and absence of vertical pane borders.

## Out of Scope And Downstream Handoff

- No Zellij/Mars border, color, transparency, tab naming, pane split, schema, validation, IO, or apply-policy change.
- No decorative box, heavy horizontal frame, shadow, or product-specific branch.
- Yazelix owns the eventual Ratconfig pin, Nix checks, installed profile, and transparent Mars light/dark dogfooding.

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` exits 0.
- Default, no-default-feature, Crossterm, and all-feature tests exit 0.
- Strict Clippy exits 0 in all-feature and no-default-feature lanes.
- Rustdoc and `git diff --check` pass.
- The rendered contract proves inset tabs/separator, padded aligned pane headings/content, a blank heading/content row, a center gutter, and zero vertical pane-border glyphs.
- Publish the accepted revision before downstream pinning.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if removing Ratconfig rails also removes a clear tab/body or settings/details hierarchy.
- Stop if fixed padding clips essential controls or Core/All state at the established 44-column boundary.
- Stop if the change alters semantic colors or dark appearance beyond geometry.
- Stop if a clean result requires host-specific border knowledge inside Ratconfig.

## Maintenance Notes

Use existing Ratatui layout and style primitives; add no dependency. The deliberately minimal separator is preferred over another enclosing frame because it establishes hierarchy without recreating the doubled host border.
notes:
Transferred from yazelix-5vl-mq4 on 2026-07-14 because the renderer palette is owned by Ratconfig. The Yazelix issue is closed as a cross-repo duplicate. Ratconfig owns implementation and publication; Yazelix owns its downstream dependency pin and product integration.

Implementation and fresh-eyes review completed locally on 2026-07-15: the light palette now uses fixed RGB muted and border roles instead of terminal-defined ANSI grays. The selected-row highlight is a background-only semantic style, fixing the prior behavior where light theming injected black foreground and overwrote selected error/status colors. Canonical dark role colors remain the compact inputs to the centralized light mapper, so dark output is unchanged and no per-widget light branches were added. Replaced the weak helper-level light test with one rendered-buffer contract covering selected invalid, normal success, muted value, title, detail metadata, help text, border, and every light foreground against both white and the selected background. Fresh eyes found and fixed a UTF-8 test bug where a byte offset was treated as a terminal-cell coordinate after multibyte box-drawing glyphs. The simplification pass removed an attempted layer of cloned semantic style wrappers after it failed the LOC/concept payoff gate; the final production diff contains only the palette correction, background-only selection owner, and deletion of the obsolete generic background mapper. No dependencies or public APIs changed. Rejected per-widget overrides, a contrast dependency, changing themed_style default-foreground semantics, and setting a foreground on the selection highlight because those options duplicate ownership, broaden behavior, or erase row semantics. Full default, no-default-features, and all-features tests plus both Clippy lanes, formatting, diff, and ledger checks pass. The initial contrast implementation is committed as dc0e8bb; a follow-up tab contrast commit, publication, Yazelix pin/integration checks, and manual Mars light-appearance dogfooding remain.

User-requested follow-up on 2026-07-15: inactive tabs now have a dedicated palette role that stays gray in dark mode and renders black in light mode; the selected tab retains the existing accent. The settings and details pane titles now use the normal text role in light mode instead of inheriting the lower-contrast border color, while dark-mode title styling remains unchanged. The two equal-width body panes overlap by one terminal cell and the later details block exact-merges its vertical border with the settings block, replacing the doubled middle border with one shared separator while keeping the outer rails flush with the body edges. As a reversible dogfooding experiment, both panes use only left/right borders: settings and details remain dedicated first-row headings, but the long top/bottom rails and junction glyphs are absent. The rendered-screen contract exercises active/inactive tabs, both pane titles, both outer rails, the single shared separator, and the absence of horizontal pane rails. No dependency, public API, product-specific policy, or unrelated color changed. Full default, no-default-features, and all-features tests plus both Clippy lanes, formatting, diff, and ledger checks pass.

Prime-beads review 2026-07-15: resolved a planning ownership contradiction. Ratconfig implementation, focused renderer verification, and publication are complete through c6ee6662013afd2d832c391d206d63ff1f6c8c1f. The remaining dependency pin, Nix checks, installed-profile refresh, transparent Mars light/dark dogfooding, and tab/body separation judgment belong exclusively to Yazelix bead yazelix-5vl-064 and are not Ratconfig closure gates.

Revised direction: no body rails; explicit tab and pane padding; a one-cell center gutter; a blank row below pane headings; and a subtle inset separator below the tabs. Preserve the contrast fixes and include the accepted successor revision in v5.0.0.

Implemented follow-up 2026-07-15, currently uncommitted: removed all body-pane borders and the exact-merge divider; replaced them with a one-cell center gutter, one-cell horizontal content insets, aligned standalone settings/details headings, one blank heading/content row, and a border-colored horizontal separator inset one cell below the tabs. Retained Ratatui Tabs’ built-in one-cell leading padding so tab text aligns with the separator and pane content without double-padding. Field columns resolve against the smaller real content width. The existing 120-column rendered-screen contract now proves semantic light-role contrast plus tabs/separator alignment, pane padding, vertical breathing room, center gutter, zero vertical body rails, and no false junctions. The 44-column Core/All contract still exposes active view, counts, toggle, search, and complete wrap-safe empty guidance. No palette, dark-mode semantic, public API, dependency, host policy, or equal pane weighting changed. Verified 98 default, 66 no-default-feature, and 102 Crossterm/all-feature tests; strict Clippy in both feature lanes; formatting; rustdoc; and diff cleanliness. Publication and downstream transparent Mars light/dark acceptance remain.

Exhaustively rendered every width 1-44 at every height 1-16 without panic, coordinate underflow, or invalid clipping, then removed that temporary probe because the supported user boundary remains the stronger 44-column contract. Replaced those self-referential expectations with direct rendered coordinates for settings/details headings and content, the blank row, center gutter, separator edges, and zero body rails. The simplification card proved identical rendered output, ordering, selection identity, styles, wrapping, side effects, public API, and feature behavior for replacing dynamic Layout builders/indexes with fixed-array vertical/horizontal layouts; deleting the resulting one-caller `body_panes` wrapper; removing unused inset parameterization; and reusing the safe horizontal inset for the header. `src/render.rs` fell from 2,560 to 2,527 lines, reducing the layout implementation diff from +58 net lines to +25 while strengthening the observable contract. Rejected inlining the named gutter constant, collapsing asymmetric title/content padding, or deleting no-rail/no-junction assertions: those save only trivia or weaken explicit user-visible boundaries. Reverified 98 default, 66 no-default-feature, and 102 Crossterm/all-feature tests; strict Clippy in both feature lanes; formatting; rustdoc; and diff cleanliness. The bead remains in progress pending commit and publication.

Second fresh-eyes and isomorphic simplification review 2026-07-15: independently re-audited separator/title adjacency, odd-width equal-pane allocation, the 44-column multi-digit Core/All boundary, one-sided title versus two-sided content padding, zero/tiny areas, tab-widget padding, Unicode separator cell accounting, theme mapping, and the explicit no-rail/no-junction contract. No further runtime defect or simplification candidate cleared the proof/payoff gate. Retained the named one-cell gutter, pane_areas, horizontal_inset, and dedicated separator renderer because each owns distinct visual policy with multiple consumers or a coherent rendering boundary. Rejected symmetric title padding because it clips the established 44-column titles; rejected a Block-based separator because it risks corner/junction output; rejected deleting direct geometry/no-rail assertions because they protect the exact user complaint. Corrected and reran the targeted narrow-header, Core/All, and rendered light-layout tests successfully. Reverified 98 default, 66 no-default-feature, and 102 Crossterm/all-feature tests; strict Clippy in both feature lanes; formatting; rustdoc; and diff cleanliness.
close_reason: Completed and published in b9309746e27e0899dd57766cf3eece3202dc76d9. Ratconfig now uses readable light-mode roles, black inactive light tabs and pane headings, no body side rails, an inset tab separator, padded pane content, a blank heading/content row, and a one-cell center gutter. The rendered contract preserves the 44-column Core/All boundary and proves contrast, spacing, no vertical rails, and no false junctions. Default, no-default-feature, Crossterm/all-feature tests, strict Clippy in both lanes, formatting, rustdoc, and diff checks passed. Yazelix still owns the dependency pin and runtime dogfooding.

title: Fix missing Rust test lane declarations
description:
## Why

Shared Yazelix agent rules require every first-party Rust file that contains `#[test]` to declare `// Test lane: ...` with an allowed lane. Several ratconfig files contain tests but lack the lane declaration, which makes the test ownership policy inconsistent and easy to miss during future edits.

## Evidence

- `src/jsonc.rs:210`: contains `mod tests` and `src/jsonc.rs:215` contains `#[test]`, but the file has no `// Test lane: ...` declaration
- `src/migration.rs:162`: contains `mod tests` and multiple `#[test]` cases, but the file has no `// Test lane: ...` declaration
- `src/toml_adapter.rs:333`: contains `mod tests` and multiple `#[test]` cases, but the file has no `// Test lane: ...` declaration
- `src/contract/mod.rs:518`: contains `mod tests` and multiple `#[test]` cases, but the file has no `// Test lane: ...` declaration
- `src/contract/toml.rs:148`: contains `mod tests` and multiple `#[test]` cases, but the file has no `// Test lane: ...` declaration
- `src/model.rs`, `src/editor.rs`, and `src/render.rs` already demonstrate the expected `// Test lane: default` file-level pattern

## Scope

In scope:
- Add the appropriate `// Test lane: default` declaration to first-party Rust files that contain governed `#[test]` functions and currently lack it
- Keep the change mechanical unless a file genuinely belongs to a different allowed lane

Out of scope:
- Do not change test behavior, assertions, fixtures, public APIs, or formatting beyond the lane declarations
- Do not add new test coverage just to touch these files
- Do not add or change dependencies

## Implementation Notes

1. Re-run `rg -n "Test lane|#\\[test\\]|mod tests" src` and confirm the current set of files with governed tests.
2. Add `// Test lane: default` near the top of each missing first-party Rust file, matching the existing style in `src/model.rs`, `src/editor.rs`, and `src/render.rs`.
3. If a tested file clearly belongs in `maintainer`, `sweep`, or `manual` instead, document that reason in the implementation notes before choosing the non-default lane.

## Verification

- `rg -n "Test lane|#\\[test\\]|mod tests" src` should show every first-party Rust file with `#[test]` has a lane declaration
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` should exit 0
- `cargo test` should exit 0

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the test lane policy has been changed or removed from the applicable `AGENTS.md` before implementation.
- Stop if the missing-lane list differs materially from the evidence and the correct lane is unclear.
- Stop if implementation appears to require behavior changes outside mechanical lane declarations.

## Maintenance Notes

- Dependency decision: no new crates; this is a mechanical policy-compliance cleanup using existing source comments only.
- Review focus: verify the lane declaration is present in every first-party Rust file with governed tests, not only the five cited examples.
close_reason: Completed: added // Test lane: default declarations to every first-party Rust file with governed tests that lacked one. Verified with rg -n 'Test lane|#\[test\]|mod tests' src, cargo fmt --all -- --check, and cargo test.

title: Unify text patch and migration outcomes
description: Replace the identical JSONC, TOML, and internal text patch outcome structs with one format-neutral PatchOutcome, and return the existing format-neutral MigrationOutcome from TOML migrations. Preserve format-specific error enums, mutation values, text output, changed() behavior, contract integration, and downstream behavior. Remove obsolete conversions and migrate known consumers during the Ratconfig 3 major-release window. Verify all feature modes plus Classic and Nova consumers; require net LOC and public-concept reduction.
notes: Dependency decision: no production or dev crates; reuse PatchMutation, the existing JSONC PatchOutcome shape, and MigrationOutcome. Implementation checkpoint 2026-07-12: moved PatchOutcome to patch.rs, re-exported it from jsonc for the existing JSONC path, made TOML patchers and both migration engines use the shared outcomes directly, removed TomlPatchOutcome, TextPatchOutcome, TomlMigrationOutcome, and all conversion glue, and migrated Classic explicit type annotations. Format-specific errors remain separate. Ratconfig production/docs diff is -75 LOC and removes three duplicate outcome concepts. Verified formatting, 88 default tests, 92 all-feature tests, 62 no-default-feature tests, strict all-target/all-feature Clippy, docs, targeted JSONC/TOML migration and contract tests, Nova 39 tests, and Classic all 390 library tests through local Ratconfig 3. PatchOutcome and MigrationOutcome are shared across adapters, obsolete duplicate outcome types and conversions are absent, and the recorded Ratconfig, Classic, and Nova verification passed with a net 75-line Ratconfig reduction. title: Add numbered tab shortcuts
description:
## Why

Cycling tabs with Tab, Shift-Tab, h, or l is predictable but slow when users repeatedly move between non-adjacent tabs. Visible numeric shortcuts would make direct navigation discoverable and reduce repeated key presses without removing the existing controls.

## Evidence

- src/editor.rs:115-128: ConfigUiApp supports only next_tab and previous_tab cycling, both of which reset selected_row to zero
- src/editor.rs:364-396: normal-mode key reduction handles cyclic tab navigation but ignores digit keys
- src/render.rs:292-305: render_tabs displays host tab labels without shortcut hints
- src/crossterm_runner.rs:45-66: unmodified character keys already map to ConfigUiKey::Char, so digit shortcuts need no new terminal abstraction

## User-Visible Contract

Render the first nine tabs as (1) label through (9) label. In normal mode, pressing 1 through 9 selects the corresponding zero-based tab position and resets row selection to the first real row. Out-of-range digits do nothing. Search and edit modes keep digits as text input. Existing Tab, Shift-Tab, h, l, arrow, and host tab behavior remain unchanged.

If a model contains more than nine tabs, only the first nine receive numeric labels and direct shortcuts; later tabs remain visible with their original labels and reachable through cyclic navigation.

## Scope

In scope:
- Add presentation-only numbering in src/render.rs without changing ConfigUiModel::tabs values or host tab identity
- Add normal-mode 1 through 9 direct selection in src/editor.rs
- Preserve search-mode and edit-mode digit entry
- Preserve existing cyclic tab navigation and selected-row reset behavior
- Add focused editor, renderer, and optional crossterm-runner regression tests
- Document numbered navigation in README.md if the public control description needs it

Out of scope:
- No multi-digit shortcuts, 0 shortcut, key chords, configurable shortcut maps, or shortcuts beyond the ninth tab
- No host-specific tab names, ordering policy, schema behavior, persistence, or runtime policy
- No public model field, intent variant, dependency, or terminal-event abstraction
- No redesign of tab styling, layout height, or overflow behavior beyond the compact numeric prefix

## Implementation Notes

1. Derive displayed numbers from tab position at render time so host-owned labels and stable tab identifiers remain untouched
2. Handle digits only after ConfigUiApp has already routed active edit and search input, ensuring numeric values and queries still work
3. Validate the requested position against model.tabs before changing selected_tab or selected_row
4. Keep the shortcut logic small and internal unless a reusable public selection API is independently justified
5. Use the existing theme and tab highlight styles for numbered labels

## Verification

- cargo fmt --all -- --check should exit 0
- cargo test should exit 0 with reducer tests for direct selection, row reset, out-of-range digits, and preserved search/edit digit input
- cargo test --all-features should exit 0 with crossterm dispatch coverage for a digit shortcut
- cargo test --no-default-features should exit 0 and retain reducer coverage without rendering
- cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings should exit 0
- git diff --check should exit 0

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if implementation requires mutating host-provided tab labels or using display text as tab identity
- Stop if digit shortcuts leak into active search or edit input handling
- Stop if supporting more than nine direct shortcuts expands into a chord or keymap subsystem
- Stop if compact prefixes cause tab rendering to require a broader overflow redesign

## Maintenance Notes

- Priority P3: this is low-risk, reusable navigation polish rather than a blocker
- Dependency decision: add no production or dev crates; use existing reducer, Ratatui Tabs rendering, and crossterm character conversion
- Packaging impact: publish a new Ratconfig revision and let downstream pinned-git hosts consume it through their normal release transaction
- Rejected alternative: alter ConfigUiModel::tabs to include numbers, because presentation metadata must not replace stable host labels
- Rejected alternative: use 0 for a tenth tab, because 1 through 9 is easier to discover and avoids a special-case mental model
close_reason: Implemented numbered labels and normal-mode 1-9 tab selection with row reset, mode-isolation regressions, crossterm dispatch coverage, and README documentation. Verified cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo test, cargo test --all-features, cargo test --no-default-features, cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings, and git diff --check.

title: Add cursor navigation to inline text editing
description:
## Why

Ratconfig's inline text editor only appends characters and removes the final character. Correcting text in the middle of a value therefore requires deleting the entire suffix or leaving the UI for the external-editor fallback. Inline editing should support walking the cursor and changing text at that position.

## Evidence

- `src/editor.rs:34-39` stores the staged input but no cursor position.
- `src/editor.rs:479-486` maps text-edit input to `String::pop`, `String::clear`, and `String::push`; `Left` and `Right` are ignored.
- `src/render.rs:1220-1234` renders `format!("{}_", edit.input)`, so the visible cursor is always fixed at the end.
- `src/render.rs:1276-1283` advertises save, cancel, clear, and external editing but no inline cursor controls.
- `README.md:220` documents `Ctrl+e` as a host-owned external-editor escape hatch; it should remain available, not be required for ordinary mid-string corrections.

## Outcome

While editing a text field, Left and Right move a visible insertion cursor through the staged value. Typed characters insert at the cursor, Backspace removes the preceding character, and existing save, cancel, clear, and external-editor behavior remains intact.

## Scope

In scope:

- Track an insertion position for `ConfigUiEditMode::Text`, initialized at the end of the current value.
- Handle Left and Right without crossing either end of the input.
- Insert `Char` input at the cursor and make Backspace remove the character immediately before it.
- Reset the cursor consistently after `Ctrl+u` and after `apply_external_text_edit`.
- Render the cursor at its true position and keep the useful portion of a long edit visible within the available terminal width.
- Keep cursor operations safe at UTF-8 character boundaries and cover non-ASCII input.
- Update the text-edit control hint and public documentation.
- Add focused reducer and rendering tests for movement, boundaries, mid-string insertion/deletion, Unicode, long-value visibility, and returned external edits.

Out of scope:

- Host validation, persistence, apply policy, temporary files, or editor launch behavior.
- Multiline editing or replacing the existing external-editor intent.
- Cursor behavior for choice and multi-choice modes.
- Mouse editing, selection, clipboard operations, undo/redo, or a general-purpose text-editor abstraction.
- Home, End, Delete, word movement, or additional public key variants in this first slice.

## Implementation Notes

1. Keep one cursor owner in Ratconfig's edit state; do not encode cursor markers inside the staged value or duplicate the buffer.
2. Store an index whose invariants are explicit and always preserve valid UTF-8 boundaries. Prefer small local helpers over a new dependency.
3. Preserve the exact staged string sent by `EditTextExternally` and `SetField`; cursor state must never leak into host intents.
4. For rendering, split or window the text around the cursor rather than appending a fake trailing cursor. The cursor must remain visible for values wider than the status area.
5. `ConfigUiEditState` is public. Before changing its construction surface, check the repository's SemVer contract and coordinate with the open v5 release bead; do not silently enlarge that release or add a compatibility shim.
6. Keep `Enter`, `Esc`, `Ctrl+u`, and `Ctrl+e` semantics unchanged.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Left and Right walk the inline cursor one character at a time and stop safely at the beginning/end.
- Typing and Backspace operate at the cursor rather than unconditionally at the end.
- ASCII and non-ASCII values cannot produce an invalid string or panic during movement/editing.
- The rendered cursor corresponds to the reducer's insertion position and remains visible when the value is clipped.
- External-editor replacement leaves the returned text ready for predictable inline editing.
- Choice and multi-choice editing behavior is unchanged.
- No new dependency is added unless terminal-cell or grapheme correctness demonstrably cannot be achieved with the current stack and the tradeoff is recorded first.

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` exits 0.
- `cargo test` exits 0.
- Focused tests prove cursor boundaries, mid-string insertion/backspace, Unicode input, external-edit replacement, and long-value rendering.
- `git diff --check` exits 0.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if cursor ownership must move into a Yazelix/Mars host; Ratconfig already owns the edit buffer, reducer, and rendering contract.
- Stop before adding public key variants, a text-editing framework, or a new Unicode dependency; record the concrete missing behavior and choose that expansion explicitly.
- Stop if a public API change would conflict with the in-progress v5 release plan; resolve release ownership before implementation or tagging.
- Stop if horizontal visibility cannot be implemented without changing unrelated detail or picker layout behavior.

## Maintenance Notes

This is a focused inline-editing slice. Preserve the external editor as the advanced path and avoid growing Ratconfig into a full text editor.
acceptance_criteria: Enter keeps a polished inline path for free-form scalar fields; normal-mode e opens the same field buffer through the host-owned external editor.
